purpose: Resolves which file wins when two SSOT files appear to disagree about the same fact. Distributed SSOT across 5 files requires explicit precedence, or conflicting reads produce silent wrong answers.

task_state_domain.governs: what task is being worked on, its status, blockers, receipts for THAT task
task_state_domain.does_not_govern: architectural facts about the system (what is implemented/wired/proven) — that is architecture_plan_fact_gap.json's domain

architecture_state_domain.governs: implementation_status, runtime_status, evidence_status, release_status of architectural areas
architecture_state_domain.does_not_govern: task queue mechanics — a task can be DONE in ACTIVE_QUEUE while its architectural area remains RELEASE_NEUTRAL pending broader verification

dependency_domain.governs: what files are required by active runtime — sole basis for archival safety

historical_migration_domain.governs: whether a historical protocol document's normative content has been migrated, and whether it is safe to archive

compliance_domain.governs: per-rule enforcement reality (declarative/code/workflow/guard) — see classification note below

precedence_order_when_files_disagree: 1. governance/proofs/<specific receipt> — a receipt is always ground truth over any narrative SSOT file; if a receipt contradicts a SSOT row, the SSOT row is wrong and must be corrected via a transaction (BEM-010 ssot_transaction_protocol.json), not trusted as-is
2. governance/roadmap/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json — wins for task_state_domain questions only
7. AGENT_CONTEXT.md and any .docx canon snapshot — NEVER wins; always lowest precedence; treated as a stale cache if it disagrees with any file above
conflict_rule: If two files claim authority over the SAME fact within the SAME domain (e.g., both architecture_plan_fact_gap.json and a stray note in ACTIVE_QUEUE.json claim a release_status for the same area), the system state is STATE_INCONSISTENT. STATE_INCONSISTENT blocks any release_status upgrade for the affected area until resolved by an explicit SSOT transaction that removes the duplicate claim from the non-owning file.
rule_enforcement_matrix_classification: DERIVED COMPLIANCE REGISTER, not primary SSOT. It is derived from (a) rule_registry.json contents and (b) governance/proofs/ receipts that test those rules. It must be regenerated/reconciled whenever rule_registry.json changes or a new enforcement receipt is created — never hand-maintained as an independent fact source.
